heading: 9.7.14.5.7.Matrix Fragments formma.m16n8k8
content:
A warp executing `mma.m16n8k8` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k8`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.f16` and `.bf16` :

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f16` / `.bf16` | A vector expression containing two `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` / `.bf16` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 71](#mma-1688-a-f16).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-1688-A-f16]
MATRIX MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

INSTRUCTION: MMA .m16n8k8
MATRIX: A (input matrix)
DATA TYPE: .f16 or .bf16
MATRIX DIMENSIONS: M=16, N=8, K=8

GRID STRUCTURE
--------------
The table has Row\Col headers.
Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 15).
Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7).
Only columns 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 are shown, but thread assignments only appear in columns 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 (pairs).

Specifically, thread assignments appear at column positions 0, 2, 4, 6 (each spanning two columns visually), corresponding to 4 thread groups per row.

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING
--------------------------

UPPER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 0 through 7):

Row 0:  col 0-1: T0:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T1:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T2:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T3:{a0, a1}
Row 1:  col 0-1: T4:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T5:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T6:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T7:{a0, a1}
Row 2:  (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 7:  col 0-1: T28:{a0, a1}  col 2-3: T29:{a0, a1}  col 4-5: T30:{a0, a1}  col 6-7: T31:{a0, a1}

LOWER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 8 through 15):

Row 8:  col 0-1: T0:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T1:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T2:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T3:{a2, a3}
Row 9:  col 0-1: T4:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T5:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T6:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T7:{a2, a3}
Row 10: (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 15: col 0-1: T28:{a2, a3}  col 2-3: T29:{a2, a3}  col 4-5: T30:{a2, a3}  col 6-7: T31:{a2, a3}

GROUPING PATTERN
----------------
Rows 0-7 map to threads T0 through T31 (one thread per pair of rows, 4 threads per row across columns 0-7).
Each thread covers 2 consecutive columns.
Rows 0-1: T0-T7 (T0,T1,T2,T3 on row 0; T4,T5,T6,T7 on row 1)
Rows 2-3: T8-T15 (implied by arrows)
Rows 4-5: T16-T23 (implied by arrows)
Rows 6-7: T24-T31 (T28,T29,T30,T31 on row 7 explicitly shown)

The same thread assignment repeats for rows 8-15, but with different register elements {a2, a3} instead of {a0, a1}.

REGISTER PACKING
----------------
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the upper half (rows 0-7): a0 and a1, packed as {a0, a1} in one 32-bit register (two f16 or bf16 values packed into a single 32-bit register).
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the lower half (rows 8-15): a2 and a3, packed as {a2, a3} in one 32-bit register.
Total per thread: 4 elements (a0, a1, a2, a3) stored in 2 registers of 32 bits each.

LABEL AT BOTTOM
---------------
%laneid:{fragments}
This indicates the notation used: lane ID maps to the listed fragment elements.

MATRIX MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

INSTRUCTION: MMA .m16n8k8
MATRIX: A (input matrix)
DATA TYPE: .f16 or .bf16
MATRIX DIMENSIONS: M=16, N=8, K=8

GRID STRUCTURE
--------------
The table has Row\Col headers.
Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 15).
Columns represent matrix columns (0 through 7).
Only columns 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 are shown, but thread assignments only appear in columns 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 (pairs).

Specifically, thread assignments appear at column positions 0, 2, 4, 6 (each spanning two columns visually), corresponding to 4 thread groups per row.

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING
--------------------------

UPPER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 0 through 7):

Row 0:  col 0-1: T0:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T1:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T2:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T3:{a0, a1}
Row 1:  col 0-1: T4:{a0, a1}   col 2-3: T5:{a0, a1}   col 4-5: T6:{a0, a1}   col 6-7: T7:{a0, a1}
Row 2:  (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 7:  col 0-1: T28:{a0, a1}  col 2-3: T29:{a0, a1}  col 4-5: T30:{a0, a1}  col 6-7: T31:{a0, a1}

LOWER HALF OF MATRIX A (rows 8 through 15):

Row 8:  col 0-1: T0:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T1:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T2:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T3:{a2, a3}
Row 9:  col 0-1: T4:{a2, a3}   col 2-3: T5:{a2, a3}   col 4-5: T6:{a2, a3}   col 6-7: T7:{a2, a3}
Row 10: (continuation pattern indicated by arrows, threads T8-T11 implied)
...
Row 15: col 0-1: T28:{a2, a3}  col 2-3: T29:{a2, a3}  col 4-5: T30:{a2, a3}  col 6-7: T31:{a2, a3}

GROUPING PATTERN
----------------
Rows 0-7 map to threads T0 through T31 (one thread per pair of rows, 4 threads per row across columns 0-7).
Each thread covers 2 consecutive columns.
Rows 0-1: T0-T7 (T0,T1,T2,T3 on row 0; T4,T5,T6,T7 on row 1)
Rows 2-3: T8-T15 (implied by arrows)
Rows 4-5: T16-T23 (implied by arrows)
Rows 6-7: T24-T31 (T28,T29,T30,T31 on row 7 explicitly shown)

The same thread assignment repeats for rows 8-15, but with different register elements {a2, a3} instead of {a0, a1}.

REGISTER PACKING
----------------
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the upper half (rows 0-7): a0 and a1, packed as {a0, a1} in one 32-bit register (two f16 or bf16 values packed into a single 32-bit register).
Each thread holds 2 register fragments for the lower half (rows 8-15): a2 and a3, packed as {a2, a3} in one 32-bit register.
Total per thread: 4 elements (a0, a1, a2, a3) stored in 2 registers of 32 bits each.

LABEL AT BOTTOM
---------------
%laneid:{fragments}
This indicates the notation used: lane ID maps to the listed fragment elements.

    >
    >
    > Figure 71 MMA .m16n8k8 fragment layout for matrix A with `.f16` / `.bf16` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:
    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row =      groupID            for a0 and a1
    >            groupID + 8        for a2 and a3
    >
    > col =  threadID_in_group * 2 + (i & 0x1)    for ai     where i = {0,..,3}
    > ```
  + `.tf32` :

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.tf32` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, containing four `.tf32` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 72](#mma-1688-a-tf32).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-1688-A-tf32]
MMA .m16n8k8 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k8
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=8
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled 0-15 (matrix rows) and columns labeled 0-7 (matrix columns).
   Rows 0-7 form the upper half; rows 8-15 form the lower half.
   Columns 0-3 form the left half; columns 4-7 form the right half.
   All 32 threads (T0-T31) participate.
   Each thread holds 4 register elements: a0, a1, a2, a3.

3. COMPLETE ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-TO-REGISTER MAPPING

   UPPER-LEFT QUADRANT (rows 0-7, columns 0-3): register element a0
     Row 0:  col 0 = T0:a0,  col 1 = T1:a0,  col 2 = T2:a0,  col 3 = T3:a0
     Row 1:  col 0 = T4:a0,  col 1 = T5:a0,  col 2 = T6:a0,  col 3 = T7:a0
     Rows 2-6: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 7:  col 0 = T28:a0, col 1 = T29:a0, col 2 = T30:a0, col 3 = T31:a0

   UPPER-RIGHT QUADRANT (rows 0-7, columns 4-7): register element a2
     Row 0:  col 4 = T0:a2,  col 5 = T1:a2,  col 6 = T2:a2,  col 7 = T3:a2
     Row 1:  col 4 = T4:a2,  col 5 = T5:a2,  col 6 = T6:a2,  col 7 = T7:a2
     Rows 2-6: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 7:  col 4 = T28:a2, col 5 = T29:a2, col 6 = T30:a2, col 7 = T31:a2

   LOWER-LEFT QUADRANT (rows 8-15, columns 0-3): register element a1
     Row 8:  col 0 = T0:a1,  col 1 = T1:a1,  col 2 = T2:a1,  col 3 = T3:a1
     Row 9:  col 0 = T4:a1,  col 1 = T5:a1,  col 2 = T6:a1,  col 3 = T7:a1
     Rows 10-14: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 15: col 0 = T28:a1, col 1 = T29:a1, col 2 = T30:a1, col 3 = T31:a1

   LOWER-RIGHT QUADRANT (rows 8-15, columns 4-7): register element a3
     Row 8:  col 4 = T0:a3,  col 5 = T1:a3,  col 6 = T2:a3,  col 7 = T3:a3
     Row 9:  col 4 = T4:a3,  col 5 = T5:a3,  col 6 = T6:a3,  col 7 = T7:a3
     Rows 10-14: pattern continues (arrows indicate continuation)
     Row 15: col 4 = T28:a3, col 5 = T29:a3, col 6 = T30:a3, col 7 = T31:a3

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per row:
     Threads T0-T3   cover row 0 (and rows 0,8 for a0/a1/a2/a3)
     Threads T4-T7   cover row 1 (and rows 1,9)
     Threads T8-T11  cover rows 2,10 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T12-T15 cover rows 3,11 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T16-T19 cover rows 4,12 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T20-T23 cover rows 5,13 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T24-T27 cover rows 6,14 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T28-T31 cover row 7 (and rows 7,15)
   Each group of 4 threads covers one matrix row across 4 columns in each half.

5. REGISTER ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD
   Each thread Tn holds exactly 4 register elements:
     a0: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a1: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a2: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread
     a3: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision).
   Each register element (a0, a1, a2, a3) holds exactly ONE f64 value.
   Since f64 is 64 bits, each element occupies two 32-bit registers (a register pair) in PTX.
   No packing of multiple values per register element occurs (unlike f16 which packs two values).

7. LANE ID NOTE
   The figure notes "%laneid:{fragments}" at the bottom, indicating the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp.

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per row:

     Threads T4-T7   cover row 1 (and rows 1,9)

5. REGISTER ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per row:
     Threads T0-T3   cover row 0 (and rows 0,8 for a0/a1/a2/a3)
     Threads T4-T7   cover row 1 (and rows 1,9)
     Threads T8-T11  cover rows 2,10 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T12-T15 cover rows 3,11 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T16-T19 cover rows 4,12 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T20-T23 cover rows 5,13 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T24-T27 cover rows 6,14 (implied by arrows)
     Threads T28-T31 cover row 7 (and rows 7,15)
   Each group of 4 threads covers one matrix row across 4 columns in each half.

5. REGISTER ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD
   Each thread Tn holds exactly 4 register elements:
     a0: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a1: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 0-3 corresponding to that thread
     a2: covers the element in rows 0-7,  columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread
     a3: covers the element in rows 8-15, columns 4-7 corresponding to that thread

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Data type is .f64 (64-bit double precision).
   Each register element (a0, a1, a2, a3) holds exactly ONE f64 value.
   Since f64 is 64 bits, each element occupies two 32-bit registers (a register pair) in PTX.
   No packing of multiple values per register element occurs (unlike f16 which packs two values).

7. LANE ID NOTE
   The figure notes "%laneid:{fragments}" at the bottom, indicating the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp.


    Figure 73 MMA .m16n8k8 fragment layout for matrix A with `.f64` type.

    The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

    ```
    groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

    row =      groupID            for a0 and a2
               groupID + 8        for a1 and a3

    col =  threadID_in_group       for a0 and a1
           threadID_in_group + 4   for a2 and a3
    ```
